role: The zoom/scale axis of the semantic instrument: it builds nested, multi-resolution cluster pyramids over persisted embedding spaces and resolves queries at a chosen coarse or unit rung.
capabilities: L2-normalize vectors and compute cosine similarity without numpy/scipy
Compute a cluster centroid as the normalized mean of its members' vectors
Build one dependency-free agglomerative dendrogram (Ward, complete, or average) with nested cuts at every K
Select the nearest available dendrogram cut for a requested K
Group a point-per-label partition into cluster label -> member index lists
Derive a dyadic default rung ladder from the space size
Compute incremental content-hash keys for centroids from member source/hash pairs
Build a small/medium scale pyramid via Ward agglomeration over persisted unit vectors
Build a large scale pyramid via a MiniBatchKMeans + Ward-over-centroids hybrid
Persist cluster centroids as queryable vectors in scale:<space>:k<K> spaces
Persist pyramid structure (membership, exemplars, cross-rung parent links) via store.save_scale_pyramid
Fetch coarse rung points (centroids, sizes, exemplars, members) for rendering
Resolve a query vector at the unit rung or a coarse rung using the existing vector_index.query_index
Fail loudly on empty, thin, missing, or inconsistent spaces/rungs
substance_why: It implements full clustering algorithms, two scalability paths, persistence contracts, and query resolution, all backed by detailed design rationale and explicit fail-loud guards.
interactions_present: build_scale_pyramid / build_scale_pyramid_kmeans invoked to construct a pyramid for a space
resolve_at_rung invoked to query a vector at a chosen unit or coarse rung
rung_points invoked to retrieve cluster centroids and metadata for a rung
load_pyramid invoked to read the persisted pyramid sidecar
interactions_missing: No wired zoom-stepping helper (next finer/coarser rung); callers must supply an explicit k
No automatic pyramid build or update trigger on corpus changes; only incremental member_hash skip
No API to delete, invalidate, or rebuild a single rung or space
No batch content-hash fetch for _member_hash; it does per-member store.get_vector round-trips
No progress or cancellation hook for long kmeans builds
No cross-rung query that automatically picks an optimal granularity
No direct UI zoom gesture; only programmatic k selection
performance_risks: Pure-Python agglomerate is O(n^3) time and O(n^2) memory with a full closest-pair scan per merge; the 4000-unit Ward threshold may already be too slow
The distance dictionary keyed by tuple pairs has high memory overhead for n in the thousands
_member_hash issues one store.get_vector per member, creating many store round-trips for large clusters
rung_points issues one store.get_vector per cluster, creating many store round-trips
The MiniBatchKMeans path materializes the full n x dim float32 matrix, so memory grows linearly with corpus size and embedding dimension
Pure-Python agglomeration over fine centroids remains O(K^3), so large top rungs can still bottleneck
reuse_why: Keep the clustering semantics, nesting contract, and scale-space addressing, but restructure the monolithic pure-Python engine and tight store coupling for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract the vector floor (_norm, _cos, centroid) into a shared vector-math module instead of replicating projection.py
Split the clustering engine (agglomerate, cut, clusters_of, default_rungs) from the persistence/build orchestrator
Isolate the scalable kmeans+ward backend behind a clustering strategy interface
Separate pyramid read/query paths (load_pyramid, rung_points, resolve_at_rung) from build/write paths
Extract scale-space addressing and the pyramid sidecar schema into a dedicated contract module
